summary:
Co-loss of BRCA2 and RB1 is not linked to better outcomes; instead, both individual losses are tied to poorer prognosis in prostate cancer due to aggressive tumor features and reduced survival. Clinical evidence supports adverse effects from these genetic alterations, making any suggestion of benefit unsupported by current research.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to two sentences as required.) 

Final Answer: B

The provided response correctly identifies that co-loss of BRCA2 and RB1 is not associated with better prognosis—it correlates with more aggressive disease and worsened survival. Both genes individually contribute to unfavorable patient outcomes when mutated or lost in prostate cancer. Final answer remains boxed as per requirement.
